findingDetails.title: Atomic save can commit index state before durable file promotion
findingDetails.summary: The atomic writer writes the pending file, commits indexPrepared/processPreparedMemory, and only then renames the pending file into place. A rename failure after the DB transaction leaves committed memory_index/ledger state for content that was never durably promoted.

findingDetails.title: Soft-delete tombstones do not tombstone chunk children
findingDetails.summary: With SPECKIT_SOFT_DELETE_TOMBSTONES=true, delete updates only memory_index.deleted_at for the requested id. Chunk children are separate rows linked by parent_id; ON DELETE CASCADE only runs on hard DELETE, not the tombstone UPDATE, and retention's tombstone purge selects only rows whose own deleted_at is set.

results.summary: Scope document names memory store/index/lifecycle write-path safety; findings are within that declared surface.
Scope folder contains no checklist.md; protocol marked not applicable for this fan-out scope artifact.
Feature-catalog comments advertise memory indexing and transaction wrappers, but two lifecycle defects remain in the sampled implementation.
Existing tests exercise indexing failure and tombstone timestamp behavior but do not cover rename-after-commit failure or chunk-child tombstone propagation.

noveltyJustification: Two new P1 data-integrity findings with concrete source citations; no prior iteration in this lineage.
ruledOut: Retention tombstone partition is intentional two-stage purge behavior; not reported as a standalone defect because tests assert it.
Background job-store table initialization is covered by context-server boot initialization; not reported.

claimAdjudication.claim: Atomic save commits index state before pending-file promotion, so a final rename failure can leave memory_index state for content that was not durably written.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked atomicIndexMemory failure handling, processPreparedMemory write transaction, create-record transaction, and atomic-index-memory tests for a post-commit promote-failure rollback; no DB rollback or cleanup path was present for promote failure after successful indexPrepared.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The retry may eventually promote the file on a later attempt, but if promotion fails persistently or the process exits after the committed index and before a successful rename, the committed row remains inconsistent.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if atomicIndexMemory moves promotion before commit or adds a transactionally verified cleanup that deletes/supersedes the committed memory row on promote failure.

claimAdjudication.claim: Soft-delete mode tombstones only the requested parent memory row and does not tombstone chunk children, leaving child rows active after deleting a chunked memory parent.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked the single-delete tombstone helper, chunk parent/child insertion, schema cascade, retention tombstone predicate, and tombstone tests. Existing tests cover repeated tombstone timestamp but not parent-child tombstone propagation.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Hard-delete mode relies on ON DELETE CASCADE, but soft-delete mode is an UPDATE, so the cascade does not apply; bulk-by-folder may tombstone children separately only when those rows are selected individually.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if soft delete explicitly cascades deleted_at to children or all retrieval/retention/projection paths prove child rows under a tombstoned parent are unreachable and later purged.
